Jon: 	You can send over in the chat, yes. If we have time to cover this, we’ll

Josh: 	A couple off-shoot questions. I’ll go ahead and take this one here. I

this really means how. “How do we get affiliates?” One of Gwen’s

affiliates. So, if you could explain that a little bit more.
Jon: 	How you get affiliates? You get a winning video sales letter. That’s
how you get affiliates.
I can tell you one of 1,000 people that I’ve coached through this whole
process, you get a winning video sales letter and affiliates will come
chasing after you. That’s just how it works.
In most industries, you have affiliates. In some industries, it’s not a big
affiliate industry; but in most industries, you have affiliates; people who
will promote your product because they know that it converts, and they
know that it’s good.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

So, if you have a good product and you have a winning video sales
letter, affiliates will come to you. One place you want to look for is
Commission Junction. ClickBank has a whole affiliate library, a
merchant library.
Obviously, you can’t contact these people. What you can do is put
your product up there, and if the product is well-converting, it’s going to
attract affiliates.
But, our course is focused primarily on creating your video sales letter,
not necessarily creating the marketing behind it.

Josh: 	Our next question here is, “Will we use all the tip versions for each
VSL, or a void for one and a how-to for another, etc?”
Jon: 	No. We’re going to go into the actual tip structure, but you’re going to
use the 3 tips in one VSL.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 7 --

3
So, your VSL tips will go inward, which we’ll cover in the next session.
We’ll cover what’s called a how-to loop; in other words, you want to
open the how-to by teasing it, and then you’re going to go right into
your void tips, right into your enjoyment tips, and you’re going to
complete with your how-to.
That is the formula that works. That’s the structure.
Again, video sales letters that work, the ideal time is 28 to 36 minutes,
right around in that area. I’ve seen video sales letters an hour and a
half long, that converted. It depends on the price point.
So, don’t worry about time. Time is the least of your concerns, when it
comes down to this. And the tips, we’ll give you specifics on like, “You
should make it about this many sentences, and this is the bullet points
here.” We’ll get into that in the next session, meaning the one coming
up after the break.
But yeah, you’ll be listing them out. Again, how-to is the tease, and
then you go into avoid, enjoy, and you close with how-to close.
Josh: 	Here’s another good question. “How do you make something
enjoyable when someone has been hurt in an injury or accident? This
is the personal injury law firm we talked about before. People that are
just interested in money.
Jon: 	You focus on what is the end goal of your client? If someone has gone
through a personal injury and they’re looking for an attorney – I’m
assuming that’s what this is – their end result is they want to enjoy a
settlement. Am I correct?
Josh: 	Yeah. Or enjoy their lives.
Jon: 	The settlement enables them to do what? The settlement enables
them to get on with their life.
What I would do is future-pace them and say, “I’m going to give you a
suggestion that a lot of our clients do after they receive their settlement
and after they move on with their life, to help you get past this moment
of pain so you can start enjoying your life again.”
And then you can look for a suggestion that you would give for your
client, that would help them do that after something positive settlement
occurred or whatever.
That may be something as simple as – I don’t know your industry, so
I’m having to make this up – but if counseling is a part of it, if bonding
with your family and being closer to your family, whatever most people
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 7 --

4
end up being after they go through this, look for one thing. That’s
definitely, like I said when we went into the formula, enjoy is hard for
some industries. This is definitely one of them that it’s difficult for.
But if you look for what your primary benefit or your second or third
benefits deliver, what kind of life would a person have after they get
this, then you could focus there, rather than enjoying the process.
For example, the weight loss thing, I can tell people how to enjoy the
process of losing weight, believe it or not. In other words, you can
make it fun. You can make it enjoyable. It’s not going to be as fun as
going to Disneyland and riding roller coasters, maybe, but it’s going to
be fun.
Something like what you’re talking about is much more challenging.
You want to focus on the end benefit of what they enjoy.
So, your enjoy tips may just be one thing that you could find. Focus on
that one thing.
And worst-case scenario, if there is nothing you can enjoy, you can just
future-pace their life at the end of this. In other words, you’ve gone
through the avoid tip, and the enjoy tip is just their future-paced life. In
other words, you can say, “Once you avoid this, this is what’s going to
happen with your life. You’re going to get this, you’re going to move on
past this settlement, you’re going to reconnect with friends and family,
get out and get moving again, you’re going to start enjoying your life
again.” And that’s enough.
Josh: 	Here’s a good one. In this case, this person owns a company that

So, he’s wondering should he make a VSL for his individual products,
or for an overview of all of his products? In this case, there could be
thousands of auto accessories in this.
Jon: 	I would say start with your overall, for sure. The cool thing about video
sales letters is that you can make smaller, shorter versions of these
video sales letters for your top 5 selling products or something It’s a
pretty wide open formula.
I would definitely start with the big picture. You can cover all of the
parts and those kinds of things. And the enjoyment tip, by the way, for
that industry would be very simple. “Here’s a way you can enjoy
refurbishing your car even more,” whatever it may be.
Josh: 	Let’s take probably one or 2 more, and then we should be good for our
break.

Jon: 	Everybody good with this? Everybody learn something? Let’s get that
out of the way first. Everybody learning something? Everybody type
“yes” if you’re learning something and if you’re enjoying this stuff.
Does everybody understand the importance of the tips now? Because
without having tips, even if you can’t get every single one of these
down, you want to get as much of them as you can down. You
definitely want the avoid tip, and you definitely need the how-to tip.
The enjoyment tip, like I said, even if it’s future-paced, that’s okay.
You’re just saying, “Here’s what your life is going to look like after you
avoid these things.” If you don’t have that structure down, trust me,
your video sales letter will crumble. Does everyone get the importance
of it?
Josh: 	We got a ton of yeses.
Jon: 	Okay, cool. Everybody cool, everybody’s learned something. Let’s

come back with a detailed segment.
Be sure to have your notes. If you’re getting hand cramps, you might

Josh: 	The next question is can tips be in video format? For example, this
person, Jonas, has a 3-instructional message video lessons. Do we
have to make these tips into PowerPoint slides instead of videos,
because he has a talking head video?
Jon: 	That’s a good question. That goes more into the advanced sections of
things, but we’ve tested talking head with video sales letter. And if you
do that correctly, it can be a total win. You have to really do it
correctly.
In other words, you want to have most of your segment with you
talking, and you have the video appearing beside you.
We’re going to go into several techniques called the paraphrase
technique in the third and final session today. You’re going to use a lot
of paraphrasing, when you get into that. And then, you’ll exit out of it
and use the straight ahead video sales letter technique where every
word is on the screen, and then you’ll come back and you’ll do the
paraphrasing bit.
So, you’ll have to set your video up like that for it to be super-
successful. That’s the formula that we’ve seen be very successful.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 7 --

6
The formula we’ve seen bomb a lot is, as a matter of fact, is when you
have video sales letter, video sales letter, video sales letter, and then
all of a sudden, talking head out of nowhere. You would think that’s a
good pattern interrupt, but it just doesn’t work. For why that doesn’t
work, everybody’s guess is as good as mine. It just doesn’t seem to
work.
So, you need to set them up ahead of time with introducing yourself,
with the whole concept right off the bat. Like if you said, “Hi, my
name’s David, and today I’m going to tell you a story about a fish.” I’m
going to keep using that example from here on out, Josh. So, you
show them the fish. “And this fish is going to help you fill in the blank,
whatever you do.”
And then he shows a picture of a fish, and you show the next slide.
“You may be thinking to yourself,” and then you see word, word, word.
You know what I’m saying? If you can visualize what I’m saying.
If you set it up like that, it can be a win. In the advanced section, I’ll
cover how to do that. It’s the same formula that we use here, it’s just
an issue of production and cameras and all of that kind of stuff. The
reason why I lean toward video sales letters for 95% of the people out
there is because of the ease in doing them and the ease in testing
them.
Once you get a winning video sales letter and it’s tested, you can add
all kinds of stuff to it. You can try the headshot combinations, you can
try animation and all of that stuff, but it all starts here. Did that answer
the question?
Josh: 	Yeah, that was good. I’ll tell you if they say yes.
Jon: 	Let’s take one more, and then we’re coming up on 11:50 and we’ll take
our 10 minute break.
Josh: 	Last question is, this just a good clarification point. “In my book, we
talk about 17 steps to better digital parenting. Should I do 17 short
VSL’s on each topic?”
Jon: 	No, I wouldn’t. You can. I’m just trying to think of your time, Dave.
That’s a lot of work.
What I would do is poll your readers, and I would take the top 3
answers to that question. In other word, what are the top 3 of the 17?
You might want to consider 3 of those, but you can also put all 3 things
of those, “Here are 3 things to make a digital parent.” That could be
your how-to tip: “3 things you absolutely have to do in order to blank.”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

then figure out which one, 3 or 5, which of these that people resonate
